what_it_does: This file is an interpretive ledger entry for the Imagery.jsx React canvas. It records a neutral summary of what the component does, lists observations, standouts, conventions, concerns, notes, questions, purpose-vs-actual assessment, themes, intention signals, novelty, contribution, and embedding-ready descriptions. It declares no code symbols.
observations: The file is a JSON interpretive layer, not source code.
It carries project, path, model, and a paragraph-length what_it_does for the Imagery canvas.

It enumerates ten observations about the canvas: four tabs/lanes, hardcoded tag vocabularies, subscription to cvImageStore and cvOpenAI, seedStarter, project creation via prompt, 360 hub editing, AI Studio delegation, shared components, fixed pano height, and cvOpenAI API shape.
It lists three standouts: cross-scope promotion, editable 360° panoramas with hotspots, and a single canvas unifying four imagery scopes.
It records four conventions: routing persistence through cvImageStore, subscribe-and-force-rerender, CvIcon/CSS vars, and window.prompt() usage.
It notes three concerns: blocking window.prompt(), swallowed upload errors, and dependence on externally-defined globals.
It includes notes, questions, purpose_vs_actual, apparent_themes, intention_signals, novelty, contribution, summary_for_embedding, and intention_for_embedding fields.
The symbols array is explicitly empty because the file contains no code symbols.
standouts: Captures a four-lane imagery canvas (system, projects, 360 hubs, AI studio) in one interpretive record.
Highlights cross-scope asset promotion and editable 360° pano hotspot authoring as distinctive design features.
Includes purpose-vs-actual matching and embedding-ready summary/intention fields for downstream indexing.
conventions: Follows the ledger schema with neutral, file-local descriptions and no ranking language.
Uses arrays for observations, standouts, conventions, concerns, questions, and themes.
Separates factual description from inferred intention and embedding text.
Leaves symbols empty when no code symbols are present.
concerns: Records evaluative concerns about the described component (blocking prompts, swallowed errors, external globals) without grounding them in visible source symbols.
Embedding summaries encode inferred intention, which may drift from the actual source over time.
The empty symbols array means the interpretation cannot be directly anchored to specific code entities in this file.
notes: Interpretive ledger entry for the Imagery.jsx canvas, summarizing its four-lane imagery subsystem, external-store dependencies, and design intent.
questions: What is the exact source code that this interpretation was derived from?
How were the external globals (cvImageStore, cvOpenAI, AIStudio, Pano360, ImageEditor) discovered and validated?
Why is the symbols array empty — was the source not parsed, or did it truly contain no symbols?
How does the embedding summary stay synchronized with source changes?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file presents itself as an interpretive summary of the Imagery.jsx canvas and contains exactly that kind of metadata.
apparent_themes: Interpretive capture of a React canvas subsystem.
intention_signals: To produce a searchable, neutral interpretive record of the Imagery canvas so downstream fusion and query can understand its scope, dependencies, and design intent without reading the source file directly.
novelty: An interpretive JSON layer that separates descriptive metadata, evaluative concerns, inferred themes, and embedding-ready summaries for a single React canvas file.
contribution: Contributes a structured interpretive snapshot of Imagery.jsx — its lanes, store contracts, conventions, concerns, themes, and embedding text — that a fused ledger can index and compare.
summary_for_embedding: An interpretive ledger JSON describing the Imagery.jsx React canvas, its four imagery lanes, cvImageStore and cvOpenAI dependencies, and design intent.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a neutral, indexable summary of the Imagery canvas for subsystem discovery and fusion analysis.
